title: How do I connect my Google Classroom account?
description: I want to link my Google Classroom to ClassBridge so my courses sync automatically.
answer: Go to your Dashboard and click the "Connect Google Classroom" button. You'll be redirected to Google to sign in and grant ClassBridge permission to read your courses and assignments. Once connected, your courses will sync automatically.

title: How do I add my child to ClassBridge?
description: I'm a parent and want to link my child's account.
answer: From your Parent Dashboard, click "Add Child" and enter your child's details. If your child already has an account, you can link by email. If not, you can create an account for them and they'll receive an invite to set their password.

title: How do I link my child's teacher?
description: I want to connect with my child's teacher for messaging.
answer: From your Dashboard, go to Messages and select your child's teacher from the contact list. If the teacher hasn't joined ClassBridge yet, you can send them an invite from the Child Profiles section.

title: Why is my Google Classroom sync failing?
description: My courses aren't syncing or I'm getting sync errors.
answer:
Google sync can fail if your connection has expired. Try these steps:
1. Go to your Dashboard
2. Click "Sync Courses" to retry
3. If that doesn't work, disconnect and reconnect Google Classroom
4. Make sure you're signing in with the same Google account linked to your Classroom

If the issue persists, your school may have changed permissions. Contact your school's IT administrator.

title: How do I sync new courses from Google Classroom?
description: I added a new course in Google Classroom but it's not showing up.
answer: New courses are synced automatically every few hours. To sync immediately, go to your Dashboard and click the "Sync Courses" button. The new course should appear within a few seconds.

title: Why don't I see my child's assignments?
description: My child's assignments from Google Classroom aren't showing.
answer:
Assignments are synced from Google Classroom through your child's account. Make sure:
1. Your child's Google Classroom is connected
2. The course is synced (click Sync Courses on their dashboard)
3. Your child is enrolled in the course in Google Classroom

As a parent, you can view your child's assignments from the Child Profiles section.

title: How do I generate a study guide?
description: I want to create a study guide from my course materials.
answer:
There are several ways to generate study materials:
1. From a Course Material: Open any course material and click "Generate Study Guide", "Generate Quiz", or "Generate Flashcards"
2. From an Assignment: Click the study tools button next to any assignment
3. Custom: From your Dashboard, click "Create Study Material" and paste or upload your own content

The AI will analyze the content and create a structured study resource for you.

title: How do I convert a study guide to a quiz or flashcards?
description: I have a study guide and want to practice with quizzes or flashcards.
answer: Open the course material that the study guide was created from, then use the tabs to switch between Study Guide, Quiz, and Flashcards. If a quiz or flashcard set hasn't been generated yet, click the "Generate" button on that tab. You can also generate them directly from the Course Materials page.

title: What happens when AI study tool generation fails?
description: I'm getting errors when trying to generate study materials.
answer:
Study material generation can occasionally fail due to:
1. Very long or complex content — try with a shorter excerpt
2. Content in an unsupported format — plain text works best
3. Temporary service issues — wait a minute and try again

If the problem persists, try creating the material from a different source or with simplified content.

title: How do I reset my password?
description: I forgot my password and need to reset it.
answer: Click "Forgot Password" on the login page and enter your email address. You'll receive an email with a link to set a new password. The link expires after 24 hours. If you don't receive the email, check your spam folder.

title: How do I switch between roles?
description: I have multiple roles (e.g., parent and teacher) and want to switch.
answer: If you have multiple roles, you'll see a role switcher next to your name in the top-right corner of the dashboard. Click on your current role to see a dropdown of your available roles, then click the one you want to switch to.

title: How do I create and assign tasks to my child?
description: I want to create tasks for my child to complete.
answer: Go to the Tasks page and click "Add Task". Enter the task title, description, priority, and due date. You can assign it to your child by selecting them from the assignee dropdown. Your child will see the task on their dashboard and can mark it complete when done.

title: How do I message my child's teacher?
description: I want to send a message to a teacher.
answer: Go to the Messages page from the navigation menu. Click "New Conversation" and select the teacher you want to message. Type your message and click Send. The teacher will receive a notification and can reply directly.

title: How do I create a course manually?
description: I want to add a course that's not in Google Classroom.
answer: Go to the Courses page and click "Create Course". Enter the course name, subject, and description. You can then add students to the course and upload course materials. Manual courses work the same as synced Google Classroom courses for study tools and tasks.

title: How do I report a bug in ClassBridge?
description: I found a problem and want to report it with a screenshot.
answer:
ClassBridge has a built-in bug report feature:
1. When you see an error dialog, click the 'Report this issue' link at the bottom of the error message
2. The Bug Report form opens with the error details pre-filled
3. You can capture a screenshot by clicking the camera icon — it takes a snapshot of your current screen
4. You can also paste a screenshot from your clipboard (Ctrl+V / Cmd+V)
5. Add a description of what you were doing when the error occurred
6. Click Submit — your report is sent to the development team as a GitHub issue with your screenshot attached

You can also open the bug report form anytime from the Help menu in the sidebar.

title: What does ClassBridge do that ChatGPT or Claude can't?
description: Understanding what makes ClassBridge different from general AI tools.
answer:
ClassBridge is fundamentally different from general AI tools in six key ways:

1. Google Classroom Integration — Auto-syncs courses, assignments, and grades directly from Google Classroom. Parents see their child's actual schoolwork without asking the kid or logging into their account. General AI tools have zero access to a student's real classroom data.

2. Role-Based Family/School Ecosystem — Parents, students, teachers, and admins each get a tailored experience. Parents can monitor multiple children, see their grades, and get AI-generated insights on actual performance. Teachers get communication tools and announcement monitoring.

3. Context-Aware Study Tools — Study guides, quizzes, and flashcards are generated from real assignments and course materials, not generic prompts. No need to copy-paste assignment details into a chatbot and craft the right prompt.

4. Parent-Teacher Messaging — Built-in communication channel between parents and teachers, integrated with the same platform where grades and assignments live. Includes Gmail/announcement monitoring for teacher communications.

5. Automated Notifications & Reminders — Assignment reminders at 8am, teacher communication sync every 15 minutes. Proactive alerts so no one has to remember to check.

6. Persistent Student Profile — Grade tracking, analytics, and AI insights over time. ChatGPT starts fresh every conversation and doesn't track your child's academic trajectory.

The value isn't the AI itself — it's the data integration + workflow automation + multi-stakeholder coordination that makes the AI actually useful in a school context without any effort from the user.

title: Will ClassBridge integrate directly with school boards and teachers?
description: Understanding upcoming school board and teacher-level integration plans.
answer: Currently, ClassBridge integrates with Google Classroom through the parent or student's own Google account. Direct integration with school boards and teacher-managed systems requires institutional permissions that are not yet in place. We are actively working on partnerships and approval processes to enable school-level and board-level integration in future phases. Stay tuned — this is a top priority on our roadmap.

title: What is the ClassBridge Wallet?
description: Understanding AI credits and the Wallet feature.
answer: The ClassBridge Wallet is your credit balance for AI-powered features. Each AI generation (study guide, quiz, flashcards, mind map) costs one credit. View your balance on the dashboard badge or the Wallet page. When credits run low, you can request more from an admin.

title: How do I take a survey on ClassBridge?
description: Participating in platform surveys and providing feedback.
answer: When a survey is available, you'll see a notification or survey card on your dashboard. Click it to open the survey, answer the questions, and submit your responses. Surveys help us improve ClassBridge based on your feedback.

title: What is Activity History?
description: Tracking your actions and usage on ClassBridge.
answer: Activity History tracks your actions on ClassBridge including study sessions, quiz attempts, material uploads, and task completions. It provides a chronological log you can filter by date range, activity type, or course.

title: What AI tools are available for parents?
description: Understanding parent-specific AI features.
answer: Parents have access to AI study tools (study guides, quizzes, flashcards, mind maps) for their children's course materials. Parents also get exclusive features like Help My Kid briefing, conversation starters, and AI-powered grade insights.

title: What are Parent Briefing Notes?
description: AI-generated academic summaries for parents.
answer: Parent Briefing Notes are AI-generated summaries that give you a quick overview of your child's academic status. They highlight upcoming deadlines, recent grades, areas needing attention, and suggested actions to support your child.

title: How do I view or download source files?
description: Accessing original uploaded documents.
answer: Open the course material detail page and go to the Document tab. Click the download icon to save the original file, or view it inline if the format is supported (PDF, images, text). Use the + menu for replace or edit options.

title: How do I ask questions about my study guide?
description: Using the Study Q&A feature to ask contextual questions about study materials.
answer:
ClassBridge has a Study Q&A feature that lets you ask questions directly about your study guide content:
1. Open any study guide from the Study page or Course Materials
2. Look for the chat icon or 'Ask a Question' button on the study guide view
3. Type your question — the AI understands the context of your specific study guide
4. Get an answer that references the actual content you're studying
5. You can save helpful AI responses as new study notes or course materials

Study Q&A uses 1 AI credit per question. The AI is contextually aware of your study guide content, so answers are specific and relevant — not generic.

title: What are XP points and badges?
description: Understanding the gamification system — experience points, badges, and study streaks.
answer:
ClassBridge rewards consistent study habits with XP (Experience Points) and Badges:

**XP Points:** You earn XP for study activities like completing quizzes, reviewing flashcards, generating study guides, and finishing tasks. View your XP balance and transaction history on the XP History page.

**Badges:** Achieve badges for milestones like your first quiz, study streaks, and more. Check the Badges page to see earned and available badges.

**Study Streaks:** Track consecutive days of study activity. Your streak appears on your dashboard. If you miss a day, you can use XP to recover your streak before it resets.

These features are available to students and help build positive study habits through achievable goals.

title: How do I change the app theme (Dark Mode)?
description: Switching between Light, Dark, and Focus themes.
answer:
ClassBridge offers three visual themes:
1. **Light** — Clean, bright default theme with teal accents
2. **Dark** — Dark background with purple/cyan accents, easier on the eyes at night
3. **Focus** — Warm muted tones designed for distraction-free study sessions

To switch themes, click the theme toggle icon in the top navigation bar (sun/moon icon). Your preference is saved automatically and persists across sessions.

title: What are sub-study guides?
description: Understanding master documents and sub-study guide hierarchy.
answer:
When you upload multiple files at once, you can designate one as the 'master document.' The master document acts as the main study guide, and each additional file becomes a sub-study guide linked to it.

Sub-study guides appear as tagged items in the course materials list with a 'Has sub study guide' indicator. You can navigate between the master and its sub-guides using the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the study guide view.

This is useful for courses with multiple chapters, units, or sections — each gets its own focused study guide while staying organized under a single master.

title: How do I print or export study materials as PDF?
description: Printing study guides, quizzes, or flashcards.
answer:
You can print or export any study material:
1. Open the study guide, quiz, or flashcard set you want to print
2. Click the Print icon (printer) or 'Download PDF' button in the toolbar
3. For printing: Your browser's print dialog opens with optimized formatting
4. For PDF: A formatted PDF file is downloaded to your device

The print view automatically adjusts formatting for paper — removing navigation elements and optimizing layout for readability.

title: Can ClassBridge read scanned or handwritten documents?
description: Understanding OCR processing for uploaded images and scanned worksheets.
answer:
Yes! ClassBridge uses OCR (Optical Character Recognition) to extract text from:
- Scanned PDF documents
- Photos of worksheets or handouts
- Images with embedded text

Simply upload the scanned document or image as a course material. ClassBridge automatically processes it and extracts the text content, which can then be used to generate study guides, quizzes, and flashcards.

For best results, ensure scanned documents are clear and well-lit. Handwritten text recognition works best with neat, legible handwriting.

title: What are Conversation Starters?
description: AI-generated discussion topics for parent-child conversations about school.
answer:
Conversation Starters are AI-generated discussion prompts designed to help parents talk with their children about school in a natural, engaging way. They're based on your child's actual courses, assignments, and recent activity on ClassBridge.

New starters are generated daily. You can find them on your Parent Dashboard or generate fresh ones on demand. Topics might include upcoming assignments, recent quiz results, or interesting course content — giving you a natural entry point for meaningful academic conversations.

title: How do I export my data from ClassBridge?
description: Downloading personal data and child information.
answer:
ClassBridge lets you download all your personal data:
1. Go to Settings from the sidebar
2. Click 'Data Export'
3. Your data is compiled and downloaded as a file containing your profile, courses, study materials, quiz results, messages, and task history

For parents, the export includes data for all linked children. This feature supports your right to access your personal information stored on the platform.

title: How do I delete my account?
description: Requesting account deletion with grace period.
answer:
To delete your ClassBridge account:
1. Go to Settings > Account Settings
2. Scroll to the bottom and click 'Delete Account'
3. Confirm your decision — you'll receive a confirmation email
4. Your account enters a 30-day grace period during which you can cancel the deletion by logging back in
5. After 30 days, your account is permanently anonymized

Important: Account deletion removes your personal data but anonymizes (rather than deletes) records to maintain data integrity for other users like teachers and classmates.

title: How do I import my calendar into ClassBridge?
description: Syncing external calendars with ClassBridge tasks and assignments.
answer:
You can import external calendars to see all your events alongside ClassBridge tasks:
1. Go to Settings > Calendar Import
2. Enter your calendar feed URL (ICS/iCal format)
3. Click 'Sync Calendar'

Imported events appear on the Tasks page calendar alongside your ClassBridge assignments and tasks. You can manually trigger a re-sync at any time. Most school and Google Calendar feeds provide an ICS URL you can copy from the calendar's sharing settings.

title: How do I view my quiz history and track improvement?
description: Reviewing past quiz attempts and score trends.
answer:
Students can track their quiz performance over time:
1. Go to Quiz History from the sidebar or Study page
2. View all past quiz attempts with scores, dates, and course information
3. See score trending — the system tracks your last 5 attempts per material and shows whether you're improving or declining
4. Click any attempt to review the questions and your answers

Quiz results are saved automatically after each attempt. Use the trend data to identify topics that need more practice.

title: What keyboard shortcuts are available?
description: Quick access to features using keyboard shortcuts.
answer: ClassBridge supports keyboard shortcuts for quick navigation. Press '?' (question mark) anywhere in the app to view the full list of available shortcuts. Common shortcuts include navigating between pages, opening search, and toggling the sidebar.

title: How do I manage my notification preferences?
description: Configuring which notifications to receive and how.
answer:
You can customize your notification settings:
1. Click the bell icon in the top navigation bar
2. Go to 'Notification Settings' or navigate to Settings > Notifications
3. Toggle individual notification types on or off (messages, assignments, reminders, system alerts)
4. Choose whether to receive email notifications for each type
5. Suppress repeated notifications of the same type if they become noisy

Email notifications are sent for important events like new messages and assignment reminders. You can unsubscribe from emails using the link at the bottom of any notification email.

title: What is the Daily Digest email?
description: Understanding the automated daily summary email.
answer:
The Daily Digest is an automated email sent each morning summarizing your day ahead on ClassBridge. It includes:
- Tasks and assignments due today
- Overdue items that need attention
- New messages received
- Recent study activity summary
- An inspirational quote

You can manage your digest preferences in Settings > Notifications. The digest helps you stay on top of your academic schedule without needing to log in.

title: How do teachers use email monitoring?
description: Understanding the Gmail integration for teacher communications.
answer:
Teachers can connect their Gmail account to ClassBridge for email monitoring:
1. Go to Teacher Communications from the sidebar
2. Click 'Connect Gmail' and authorize access
3. ClassBridge syncs your inbox and shows relevant parent/student emails
4. AI generates summaries of key communications
5. Reply to emails directly from ClassBridge

Email monitoring helps teachers keep track of parent communications alongside their ClassBridge messages. Sync happens automatically, and you can trigger a manual sync at any time.

title: How do I upload multiple files at once?
description: Using multi-file upload with master document selection.
answer:
ClassBridge supports uploading up to 10 files at once:
1. Click 'Upload Materials' from your dashboard or Course Materials page
2. Select multiple files (PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, images, text) — max 20MB each
3. If uploading multiple files, you can designate one as the 'master document' that the others relate to
4. Choose which AI tools to generate during upload (Study Guide, Quiz, Flashcards)
5. Select the target course and click Upload

You can also paste text directly instead of uploading a file. Generation happens in the background — you can continue working while AI processes your materials.

title: How does the study streak work?
description: Understanding study streaks and streak recovery.
answer:
Study streaks track consecutive days of study activity on ClassBridge:
- Your streak increases by 1 for each day you complete a study activity (take a quiz, review flashcards, read a study guide)
- Your current streak is displayed on the student dashboard
- If you miss a day, your streak resets to 0
- You can use earned XP points to recover a broken streak before it resets
- School holidays (set by admins) don't break your streak

Streaks encourage consistent daily study habits and work alongside the XP and badge system.
